batch_summary: Deed [324]: Affidavit of Heirship for estate of Gussie Weaver, filed by daughter Josie Evelyn Weaver in Freestone County (FC Doc 1985-5299, OR/699/677, filed 8/21/1985). Identifies Gussie's sole heirs: wife Jewell P. Weaver and two children, Josie Evelyn Weaver (b. 3/8/1915) and Ted A. Weaver (b. 12/19/1933). Gussie died intestate 8/7/1975; no probate; estate ~$25,000 (cash and land). Property described is two Longbotham tracts near Wortham (67.2 ac + 12 ac) — NOT the Weaver Tract F15408, but the heirship information directly resolves the 'Relationship to Gussie and Jewell TBD' question for Josie Evelyn Weaver and confirms Ted A. Weaver as Gussie's son. ISSUE-02 advanced: Gussie Weaver heir line now fully identified; Jewell Weaver heir line still requires independent tracing. Property chain: T. Elmo Longbotham → Gussie Weaver et ux (1930, FC Vol. 107, Pg. 587) → Jewell P. Weaver by intestacy (1975) → Ted A. Weaver by deed (12/23/1980, FC Vol. 693, Pg. 131). Metes-and-bounds reference J.V. Bounds W. line, placing Longbotham tracts adjacent to Bounds/Richie League holdings.

02_PROPERTY_INVENTORY.md.timestamp_summary: Deed [324] — Weaver Tract mineral owner correction updated; Gussie Weaver heir line identified per heirship affidavit

operations.description: Update Weaver Tract Mineral Owner Correction to reflect that Gussie's heirs are now identified via deed [324]
operations.find: Previous inventory noted "Weaver heirs own minerals." The correct attribution is **Gussie Weaver heirs and Jewell Weaver heirs jointly** — both were grantors in Deed [03] (1955) and the mineral reservation language is plural: "reserved to the grantors herein." Both Weaver lines must be identified to account for the full mineral interest.
operations.replace_with:
Previous inventory noted "Weaver heirs own minerals." The correct attribution is **Gussie Weaver heirs and Jewell Weaver heirs jointly** — both were grantors in Deed [03] (1955) and the mineral reservation language is plural: "reserved to the grantors herein." Both Weaver lines must be identified to account for the full mineral interest.

**Gussie Weaver heir line — IDENTIFIED** [PDF CONFIRMED: deed [324], 2026-03-31]: Gussie Weaver died intestate 8/7/1975. Married once only to Jewell P. Weaver (3/4/1914, Richland TX). Two children: Josie Evelyn Weaver (b. 3/8/1915, deceased by 2012) and Ted A. Weaver (b. 12/19/1933). Under 1975 Texas intestacy law, Gussie's undivided community interest in the reserved minerals passed to his children (Jewell retained her own community half). The Jewell Weaver heir line requires separate identification — her minerals are held in the Weaver Jewell P Family Testamentary Trust.

03_DEED_CHAIN_SUMMARY.md.timestamp_summary: Deed [324] added to Phase 2 — Affidavit of Heirship, Josie Evelyn Weaver re: estate of Gussie Weaver; Gussie heir line identified (Jewell, Josie, Ted); ISSUE-02 advanced

operations.description: Add deed [324] entry to Phase 2 section, after deed [03] (the Weaver→JP Black surface deed that created the mineral severance)
operations.anchor: 1. `1955-09-13_[03]_f` — Gussie Weaver et ux → JP Black (warranty, **surface only**, Freestone) → F15408 (Weaver tract, minerals permanently severed by both Gussie Weaver and wife Jewell Weaver)
operations.content: 1a. `1985-08-21_[324]_f` — Josie Evelyn Weaver (affiant, Navarro County) → Public (affidavit of heirship, Freestone) — **Estate of Gussie Weaver**: Gussie died intestate 8/7/1975; married once only to Jewell P. Weaver (3/4/1914, Richland TX); two children only: Josie Evelyn Weaver (b. 3/8/1915) and Ted A. Weaver (b. 12/19/1933). No probate; estate ~$25,000 (cash and land). Property described: two Longbotham tracts near Wortham (First Tract 67.2 ac, Second Tract 12 ac) — NOT the Weaver Tract F15408 but the heirship information identifies Gussie's heirs for the mineral ownership question (ISSUE-02). Source deed: T. Elmo Longbotham + wife Earle Longbotham → Gussie Weaver + wife Jewell Weaver, FC Vol. 107, Pg. 587 (Jan 8, 1930). Chain: Gussie (1930–1975) → Jewell by intestacy (1975–1980) → Ted A. Weaver by deed (12/23/1980, FC Vol. 693, Pg. 131). Metes-and-bounds First Tract calls J.V. Bounds W. line — Longbotham tracts adjacent to Bounds/Richie League holdings. County struck through "FREESTONE" and replaced with "FREESTONE" (affiant hand-corrected county of residence to NAVARRO, initialed J.E.W.). Notarized Navarro County before Dawn J. Coley NP (comm. exp. 5/6/1989). FC Doc# 1985-5299, Vol. 699, Pg. 677–679 (3 pages). Filed Aug 21, 1985 at 3:30 PM by Doris Terry Welch, Clerk, Freestone County (Deputy: Michelle Jones). ⚠️ Name variant: affidavit spells grantor of source deed as "T. Elmo Lonbotham" (missing 'g') in recital but "T. Elmo Longbotham" and "T.E. Longbotham" elsewhere — scrivener's error in one instance. ISSUE-02 PRIORITY PDF PULL satisfied. Phase 2

operations.description: Add deed [324] analysis entry to ISSUE-02 evidence section, after the leasing history line
operations.anchor: - **Leasing history on Weaver Tract minerals**: 1950 Gussie→Bounds BF; 1961 Gussie→Meeks (33.61 ac); 1978 Jewell→Hoover (33.11 ac); 1982 Amoco release to Jewell; 2012 Jewell P Trust + Josie E U/W/O → Polomsky Hughes (3 memoranda).
operations.content: - **Deed [324] analysis (2026-03-31)**: Affidavit of Heirship (FC Doc 1985-5299, OR/699/677–679, filed 8/21/1985). Affiant Josie Evelyn Weaver (Navarro County resident) attests: Gussie Weaver was her father; died intestate 8/7/1975; married once only to Jewell P. Weaver (3/4/1914, Richland TX); two children only: Josie Evelyn Weaver (b. 3/8/1915) and Ted A. Weaver (b. 12/19/1933); estate ~$25,000 (cash + land); no probate. Property described: TWO LONGBOTHAM TRACTS near Wortham (First Tract 67.2 ac, Second Tract 12 ac) — NOT the Weaver Tract F15408, not in Richie League. Source deed: T. Elmo Longbotham + wife Earle Longbotham → Gussie Weaver + wife Jewell Weaver, FC Vol. 107, Pg. 587 (1/8/1930; also cited as Vol. 107, Pg. 582). First Tract metes-and-bounds call "J.V. Bounds W. line" — placing Longbotham tracts adjacent to Bounds/Richie League holdings. Chain of possession: Gussie (1930, farming/ranching) → Jewell by intestacy (1975, farming/ranching) → Ted A. Weaver by deed 12/23/1980 (FC Vol. 693, Pg. 131, recorded 1/17/1985). Weaver family claimed adverse possession from 1930 through filing date. **Key resolution**: Josie Evelyn Weaver is confirmed as Gussie and Jewell's daughter — resolves the "Relationship to Gussie and Jewell TBD" question in both file 04 and file 05. **Mineral interest implications**: The affidavit does not address the F15408 mineral reservation, but under 1975 Texas intestacy law, Gussie's undivided community interest in the deed [03] reserved minerals would have passed to his children (Josie 1/2, Ted 1/2 of Gussie's share), with Jewell retaining her own community half. Exact fractional interests depend on whether the mineral reservation was community or separate property — requires legal analysis. **Remaining ISSUE-02 work**: (a) Jewell Weaver heir line still unidentified — her minerals are held in the Weaver Jewell P Family Testamentary Trust; pull FC 2012-1204330 (trust lease memorandum) to identify trustee/beneficiaries; (b) two supporting affidavits (FC 1985-5298, 1985-5297) still pending pull; (c) Josie's 1948 Richie Survey interest (FC Doc 1948-67) predates this affidavit by 37 years — she held independent mineral interests before Gussie's death.

operations.content: | [324] | Josie Evelyn Weaver (affiant) → Public (re: estate of Gussie Weaver heirship) | Freestone | ✓ | FC Doc# 1985-5299, Vol. 699, Pg. 677–679. Filed Aug 21, 1985 at 3:30 PM; Doris Terry Welch, Clerk, County Court, Freestone County; Deputy: Michelle Jones. | 677–679 (3 pages) | — | 1985-08-21 | **Affidavit of Heirship** — Estate of Gussie Weaver. Affiant Josie Evelyn Weaver (Navarro County resident) attests: Gussie Weaver was her father; died intestate 8/7/1975; married once only to Jewell P. Weaver (3/4/1914, Richland TX); two children only: Josie Evelyn Weaver (b. 3/8/1915) and Ted A. Weaver (b. 12/19/1933); no probate; estate ~$25,000 (cash + land). Property: two Longbotham tracts near Wortham, Freestone County — First Tract 67.2 ac (inherited by T. Elmo Longbotham from deceased father T.E. Longbotham; metes-and-bounds call J.V. Bounds W. line), Second Tract 12 ac (T.E. Longbotham homestead, west limits of City of Wortham). Source deed: T. Elmo Longbotham + wife Earle Longbotham → Gussie Weaver + wife Jewell Weaver, FC Vol. 107, Pg. 587 (1/8/1930; also cited as Vol. 107, Pg. 582 for "Gussie Weaver, et ux" recording). Chain: Gussie (1930–d.1975) → Jewell by intestacy (1975–1980) → Ted A. Weaver by deed 12/23/1980 (FC Vol. 693, Pg. 131, recorded 1/17/1985). Adverse possession claimed 1930–1985 (farming/ranching, fenced, taxes paid). County heading originally typed "FREESTONE" then struck through and retyped "FREESTONE" — affiant hand-corrected county of residence to "NAVARRO" with initials "J.E.W." Notarized Navarro County before Dawn J. Coley NP, State of Texas (comm. exp. 5/6/1989). ⚠️ Name variant: "T. Elmo Lonbotham" (missing 'g') in one instance vs "T. Elmo Longbotham" and "T.E. Longbotham" elsewhere — scrivener's error. No conveyance — heirship documentation only. **Resolves ISSUE-02 Gussie Weaver heir identification**; property described is NOT the Weaver Tract F15408 but identifies Gussie's heirs for the mineral ownership question. ISSUE-02 PRIORITY PDF PULL satisfied. Phase 2 |
